$description: Structured knowledge base for the Piper PA-28-140 Cherokee 140 (Cherokee Cruiser). Compiled from POH part No. 761 555, issued July 1973, revisions through 1984. Values hand-verified against source page scans. Intended for AI agent retrieval and in-cockpit reference apps.
$disclaimer: Advisory only. The current, on-board, FAA-approved Airplane Flight Manual and Weight & Balance Report are the legal source of truth for flight. Cross-check any critical value against them before use.

aircraft.acrobatic_maneuvers: prohibited in normal category; only utility-category maneuvers allowed in utility category

engine.carburetor_heat: recommended any time throttle is closed at or below cruise power

fuel.notes: Verify unusable fuel from current W&B. Boost pump ON for takeoff and landing.

weights.cg_range_note: Datum is 78.4 in ahead of wing leading edge at straight/tapered intersection. See W&B envelope pages 91, 93, 94 for exact limits at your weight.

v_speeds_cas.$note: POH is authored in MPH. Kts included for convenience. All values are Calibrated Airspeed.

Vso.meaning: Stall speed in landing configuration (flaps down, power off)

Vs.meaning: Stall speed clean (flaps up, power off)

Vglide.meaning: Best glide (air conditioner off, from Emergency Procedures)

Va.meaning: Design maneuvering at gross weight (2150 lb). Reduce with weight.

Vno.meaning: Maximum structural cruising (top of green arc)

stall_speeds_by_bank_mph_cas.$description: Power-off stall speeds in MPH CAS by angle of bank
stall_speeds_by_bank_mph_cas.altitude_loss_during_stall_ft: up to 200 (depends on configuration and power)

performance_sea_level_2150_lb.note_wheel_fairings: All speeds above assume optional wheel fairings installed; subtract 3 mph if not installed.

cruise_performance.$note: TAS in MPH, ranges in statute miles using 48 gal usable, no reserve.

best_power_mixture.note: range only; TAS interpolated in POH

engine_failure_takeoff_runway_remaining.notes: Do not attempt turnback at low altitude.

memory_items: Airspeed - maintain safe airspeed (do NOT stall trying to turn back)
Turns - shallow only, to avoid obstacles
Flaps - as required, typically full for touchdown
Land straight ahead with minor course changes only
engine_failure_takeoff_no_runway.notes: If sufficient altitude was gained, attempt restart per engine failure in flight procedure.

memory_items: Airspeed - 80 MPH IAS (best glide, A/C off)
Fuel selector - switch to another tank containing fuel
Engine gauges - check for cause of power loss
If no fuel pressure - verify tank selection is on tank containing fuel
When power restored: Carb heat OFF, Electric fuel pump OFF
if_time_permits: Ignition switch - L, then R, then back to BOTH
Throttle and mixture - try different settings
Try other fuel tanks (water in fuel may take time to clear)
engine_failure_in_flight_restart.notes: If failure was fuel exhaustion, power will not restore until empty lines are refilled — up to 10 seconds after switching tanks.

memory_items: Airspeed - 80 MPH IAS (best glide, A/C off)
Field - select suitable, head into wind if possible
Notify FAA by radio (121.5 or ATC) of intentions
Seat belts and shoulder harness - TIGHT
Touch down at minimum controllable airspeed
power_off_landing.notes: Establish spiral over the field, aim to be at 1000 ft AGL on downwind. Excess altitude with pattern extension, flaps, or slip.

memory_items: Continue cranking to draw fire into engine
If fire persists: Master switch OFF, evacuate airplane
Cabin heat and defrost - OFF (unless needed to clear smoke)
Establish glide, proceed with power-off landing
Land at nearest suitable airport as soon as possible
Reduce power to minimum needed to reach the field
Prepare for possible engine seizure or forced landing en route
Fuel selector - verify on tank with fuel
If pressure not restored, prepare for engine failure and land as soon as practical
Land as soon as practical if temperature stays in yellow/red
Reduce electrical load immediately (nonessential equipment OFF)
Verify with ammeter and warning light
Master switch - cycle OFF then ON to attempt reset
If not restored: land as soon as practical to preserve battery for flaps, radio, gear indication
Carburetor heat - ON (check for carb ice)
If roughness persists: land as soon as practical
When rotation stops: rudder neutral, ease out of dive
spin_recovery.notes: Spins prohibited in Normal category. Placarded: 'FOR SPIN RECOVERY, USE FULL RUDDER AGAINST SPIN, FOLLOWED IMMEDIATELY BY FORWARD WHEEL.'

memory_items: Continue flying the airplane — door is not a control emergency
Yaw the airplane to help close, or land and reclose on the ground
Do not lean out to close in flight

steps: Release seat belt securing control wheel
Master switch - ON, check fuel quantity (both tanks)
Master switch and ignition - OFF
Check for external damage, control surface interference
Wings and control surfaces free of snow, ice, frost
Visually check fuel supply in both tanks, caps secure
Drain fuel system sumps and gascolator; check for water/sediment/proper grade
Check fuel system vents are open
Check landing gear shock struts (main ~4.5 in extension)
Check tires for cuts, wear, inflation
Check brake pads and discs for wear
Inspect windshield for cleanliness
Check propeller and spinner for nicks and defects
Check for fuel or oil leaks
Check oil level: 8 quart maximum, dipstick fully seated
Inspect cowling and inspection covers for security
Check nose wheel tire and shock strut (~3.25 in extension)
Check air inlets clear
Check alternator belt tension
Stow tow bar and control locks
Check baggage stowed and secured
Verify required papers are in aircraft
Fasten seat belts and shoulder harness; check inertia reel
Parking brake - ON
Carburetor heat - FULL COLD (OFF)
Fuel selector - select desired tank
Circuit breakers - all IN
Mixture - IDLE CUT-OFF
Master switch - ON
Electric fuel pump - ON for 3 seconds, then OFF
Throttle - cracked (approx 1/4 inch)
Mixture - RICH
Magneto switch - START (rotate clockwise and press)
When engine fires: retard throttle to 800 RPM
Verify oil pressure within 30 seconds — if not, shut down and investigate
starting_engine_cold.notes: Cold weather: allow a few extra seconds for oil pressure indication. Cranking limit: 30 seconds on, 2 minutes off.

steps: Throttle - open approximately 1/2 inch
Mixture - IDLE CUT-OFF
Master switch - ON
Magneto - START
When engine fires: mixture RICH slowly, throttle 800 RPM
Throttle - FULL OPEN
Mixture - IDLE CUT-OFF
Master switch - ON
Magneto - START
When engine fires: quickly retard throttle, mixture RICH
Master switch - OFF
Connect RED lead to POSITIVE (+) of external 12V battery
Connect BLACK lead to NEGATIVE (-)
Insert plug into fuselage receptacle
Master switch - ON, proceed with normal start
After start: Master OFF, disconnect jumper cable
Master ON, verify alternator output on ammeter - DO NOT FLY WITHOUT ALTERNATOR OUTPUT
Warm up at 800-1200 RPM
Avoid high power runs on the ground
Taxi with brakes tested before increasing throttle
Steer with rudder pedals (nose wheel is steerable through pedals)
Face into wind, brakes set
Throttle - 2000 RPM
Magneto check: switch to L, note RPM drop; back to BOTH; to R, note drop; back to BOTH
Max drop 175 RPM; max differential between L and R: 50 RPM
Carburetor heat - ON, note RPM drop (confirms hot air); return OFF
Vacuum gauge - check in green
Ammeter - check charging
Suction - check in operating range
Idle check at 600-700 RPM
Mixture - lean and enrich to check for smooth idle
Fuel selector - fullest tank
Electric fuel pump - ON
Mixture - RICH (lean at high density altitude)
Carburetor heat - COLD
Trim - set for takeoff
Flaps - as required (0 or 25 for short field)
Controls - free and correct
Engine gauges - green
Doors and windows - closed and latched
Seat belts and shoulder harness - fastened and tight
Line up, apply full throttle smoothly
Verify engine parameters and full RPM
Rotate at approximately 65 MPH IAS
Climb at Vy = 85 MPH for best rate, or Vx = 74 MPH if obstacle
Electric fuel pump - OFF at safe altitude and verify fuel pressure remains
Flaps - retract in steps if used
Landing gear - N/A (fixed gear)
Best rate: 85 MPH IAS
Best angle: 74 MPH IAS
Cruise climb: 95-100 MPH IAS for visibility and engine cooling
Monitor cylinder head and oil temperatures
Lean mixture above 3000 ft density altitude for smooth operation
Set cruise power (65-75% typical for economy)
Lean mixture per POH cruise chart or by roughness/EGT
Monitor fuel gauges, oil temp/pressure, fuel pressure
Switch fuel tanks periodically to keep laterally balanced
Carburetor heat - ON any time throttle is closed or below cruise power
Mixture - RICH as you descend below 5000 ft density altitude
Fuel selector - fullest tank
Electric fuel pump - ON before entering pattern
Pattern speeds: 90 MPH downwind, 80 MPH base, 75 MPH final
Full flaps by short final if runway allows
Over the fence: approximately 70 MPH
Cross fence at ~70 MPH
Reduce power to idle in flare
Hold nose off, touch down mains first
Lower nose wheel gently after mains are down
Apply brakes as needed
Flaps - retract only after clear of runway
Carburetor heat - COLD after landing
Flaps - UP
Carburetor heat - COLD
Electric fuel pump - OFF
Idle at 1000 RPM for at least 1 minute to cool
Mixture - IDLE CUT-OFF to stop engine
Magnetos - OFF (after propeller stops)
Master switch - OFF
Fuel selector - LEFT or as needed
Control lock - installed
Chocks and tie-downs - as needed

operating_tips.carb_ice: Cherokee 140's Lycoming O-320 is carb-ice prone in humid conditions from ~20°F to 90°F OAT. Use full carb heat any time throttle is at or below cruise power.
operating_tips.fuel_management: Alternate tanks every 30 minutes in cruise to keep laterally balanced. Do not use lowest tank on takeoff or landing.
operating_tips.leaning: Above 5000 ft density altitude or 75% power below that, lean for smoothness or per EGT.
operating_tips.spin_prohibition: Spins prohibited in Normal category. Utility category spins allowed only with flaps up.
operating_tips.load_planning: At gross weight (2150), aft CG is critical — verify seat position and baggage placement.
operating_tips.engine_hour_use: Monitor engine hour meter for oil change intervals (typically 50 hr / calendar quarter).

conversions.$note: The POH is in MPH; modern instruments and radio calls often use knots. This block is here for the agent to convert cleanly.

sources.$note: For any value that seems wrong, cross-check against the original page image. Every JPEG below is a scan of the printed page.
